hero.headline: Nurturing Growth, One Family at a Time
hero.subheadline: Is your child struggling with development, behavior, or school challenges? I help families find practical, expert solutions — online or in person.

services.sectionSubtitle: Tailored support designed around the unique needs of your child and family.

individual.title: Individual Consultation
individual.description: One-on-one sessions with parents or families, online or in person. Focused on your specific questions and challenges.

workshops.title: Group Workshops
workshops.description: Interactive workshops on parenting, child development, and family dynamics. Small groups for optimal engagement.

assessment.title: Child Assessment
assessment.description: Professional developmental assessments for children. Insights into strengths and areas for targeted support.

school.title: School & Educator Support
school.description: Support for schools, teachers, and educational institutions on pedagogical challenges and classroom management.

trust.sectionSubtitle: What parents say about the support

1.quote: Within 3 sessions, communication at home transformed completely. We understand our daughter so much better now — fewer conflicts, more connection.
1.author: Marieke V.

2.quote: The boundary-setting workshop was a turning point. In one afternoon we finally had a framework that actually works with our 6-year-old.
2.author: Thomas & Lena B.

aboutTeaser.bio: With over ten years of experience in pedagogical guidance, I help children and families with their development. My approach is warm, practical, and always tailored to your unique situation.

cta.headline: Ready to Take the First Step?
cta.subheadline: Get in touch today for a free introductory conversation.

faq.sectionSubtitle: Everything you need to know before reaching out

1.question: How much do sessions cost?
1.answer: Session rates vary depending on the service and whether it is online or in person. Contact me for a personalized quote — the introductory call is always free.

2.question: I'm not sure which service is right for us. Can you help?
2.answer: Absolutely. During the free introductory call we discuss your situation and I recommend the most suitable approach. There is no pressure to commit.

3.question: Do you work online or only in person?
3.answer: Both. Individual consultations, group workshops, and school support are available online. Child assessments are conducted in person only.

4.question: How quickly can I get an appointment?
4.answer: I typically respond within 24 hours and can usually schedule an introductory call within a few days.

5.question: What ages do you work with?
5.answer: I work with children from early childhood through adolescence, and with their families and educators throughout that journey.

biography.title: My Story
biography.paragraph1: As a pedagogue and family counselor, I have contributed to the development of children and the strength of families for over ten years. My passion for this work began during my studies in Pedagogical Sciences, where I specialized in child and family psychology.
biography.paragraph2: Since then, I have guided hundreds of families through diverse challenges: from behavioral issues and developmental delays to school difficulties and family dynamics. I strongly believe in the strength of every family and work from a positive, solution-oriented approach.
biography.pullQuote: Every child has their own pace. My role is to respect that pace and support the child at every step.

philosophy.title: My Approach
philosophy.subtitle: Three core principles that define my work with children and families

pillar1.title: Child-Led
pillar1.description: The child is at the center. I connect with the needs, pace, and strengths of each individual child.

pillar2.title: Family-Centered
pillar2.description: Parents and caregivers are the experts on their own child. I work with the whole family as a system.

pillar3.title: Evidence-Based
pillar3.description: My approach is grounded in current scientific research and proven effective methods.

about.ctaText: Feel free to reach out for a free introductory conversation.

footer.tagline: Professional pedagogical guidance for children and families.

privacy.intro: Your privacy is important to us. This privacy policy explains what personal data we collect, why we use it, and how we handle it.

collection.title: What data do we collect?
collection.content: We collect personal data that you actively provide to us, such as your name, email address, and phone number via the contact form. We do not collect data through cookies or tracking technologies.

use.title: How do we use your data?
use.content: Your data is used solely to contact you in response to your message or appointment request. We never sell or share your data with third parties.

storage.title: How long do we retain your data?
storage.content: We retain your personal data no longer than necessary for the purpose for which it was provided, with a maximum of two years.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.content: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. Please contact us via the contact form to exercise these rights.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: For questions about this privacy policy, please contact us at info@pedagogischadvies.nl.

terms.intro: These terms of service apply to all services offered through this website.

services.title: Services
services.content: We offer pedagogical guidance and advice for children and families. The specific content and duration of services are determined in consultation.

payment.title: Payment
payment.content: Payment arrangements are confirmed in writing prior to the commencement of services. Invoices must be paid within 14 days of the invoice date.

cancellation.title: Cancellation
cancellation.content: Appointments may be cancelled free of charge up to 24 hours in advance. Later cancellations will be charged at 50% of the agreed rate.

liability.title: Liability
liability.content: We are not liable for indirect damages arising from the use of our services. Our liability is limited to the amount of the service provided.

disputes.title: Disputes
disputes.content: Dutch law applies to these terms. Disputes are preferably resolved through mutual consultation.

servicesPage.pageSubtitle: Tailored pedagogical support designed around the unique needs of your child and family.
servicesPage.intro: Every child and family is unique. That's why I offer a range of support options — from one-on-one consultations to group workshops and professional child assessments. Explore the services below to find the right fit for your situation.

individual.tagline: One-on-one support tailored to your family's unique needs
individual.description: In a private individual consultation, we focus entirely on your specific situation. Whether you have questions about your child's development, behavior, or school challenges — or whether you need guidance as a parent — these sessions give you the time and space to explore practical solutions together.

whatToExpect.title: What to Expect
whatToExpect.item1: A warm, non-judgmental conversation focused on your situation
whatToExpect.item2: Practical insights and tools you can apply right away

whoIsItFor.title: Who Is This For?
whoIsItFor.text: Parents and caregivers seeking personal guidance, families navigating behavioral or developmental challenges, and anyone looking for a confidential space to discuss their child's wellbeing.

cta.title: Ready to Take the First Step?
cta.text: Book your free introductory call — no commitment required.

workshops.tagline: Learn, share, and grow alongside other families
workshops.description: Our group workshops bring together parents and caregivers in a supportive setting to explore topics in child development, parenting strategies, and family dynamics. Small group sizes ensure every participant receives meaningful attention and space to ask questions.

whatToExpect.title: What to Expect
whatToExpect.item1: Interactive sessions with fellow parents in a small group setting
whatToExpect.item2: Evidence-based strategies presented in accessible, practical language
whatToExpect.item3: Take-home exercises and tools to continue the work at home

whoIsItFor.title: Who Is This For?
whoIsItFor.text: Parents who enjoy learning in a group setting, caregivers wanting to connect with others facing similar challenges, and schools or organizations looking to offer structured parenting support.

cta.title: Interested in a Workshop?
cta.text: Get in touch to find out about upcoming group sessions.

assessment.tagline: In-depth insight into your child's development and strengths
assessment.description: A professional developmental assessment provides a clear, evidence-based picture of where your child is in their development — their strengths, areas for growth, and any factors that may benefit from additional support. All assessments are conducted in person.

whatToExpect.title: What to Expect
whatToExpect.item1: A thorough, structured observation and evaluation session
whatToExpect.item2: A detailed written report with findings and actionable recommendations
whatToExpect.item3: A follow-up consultation to discuss results and plan next steps

whoIsItFor.title: Who Is This For?
whoIsItFor.text: Parents concerned about their child's development, schools or specialists seeking an independent professional assessment, and families wanting clarity before accessing further support services.

cta.title: Want to Know More?
cta.text: Get in touch to discuss whether an assessment is right for your child.

school.tagline: Empowering educators to support every child's development
school.description: This service is designed for educational professionals — teachers, school counselors, and administrators — who want to better understand and respond to the diverse developmental needs of their students. I work directly with schools to build knowledge, confidence, and practical strategies.

whatToExpect.title: What to Expect
whatToExpect.item1: On-site or online consultations with teaching staff and school teams
whatToExpect.item2: Practical strategies for classroom management and inclusive teaching
whatToExpect.item3: Tailored workshops and training sessions for your school's specific needs

whoIsItFor.title: Who Is This For?
whoIsItFor.text: Teachers facing classroom challenges, school counselors seeking specialist input, and educational institutions looking to strengthen their support for students with developmental or behavioral needs.

cta.title: Working in Education?
cta.text: Reach out to explore how I can support your school or team.
